title: The "Fair Armenia" party issues an official statement on parliamentary elections
content: The Fair Armenia party reflected on the snap parliamentary election results as a key message about human relations during political activity and called for assessing internal and external challenges facing Armenia through the collective potential of the nation.

title: Professor Armen Charchyan detained by police
content: Professor Armen Charchyan was detained by police in Armenia. The article covers the circumstances of the detention.

title: On one hand, Aliyev keeps stating that they're ready to talk with NK Armenians, on the other hand, they attack - Edmon Marukyan
content: RA Ambassador-at-Large Edmon Marukyan referred to the Armenian-Azerbaijani conflict, discussing Azerbaijan's contradictory stance on negotiations with Nagorno-Karabakh Armenians while simultaneously attacking them.

title: There will be some actions, there will be answers - Zakharova announced that Russian peacekeepers will respond to the appearance of representatives of the EU mission
content: Maria Zakharova, the representative of the Russian Foreign Ministry, announced that Russian peacekeepers will respond to the appearance of representatives of the EU mission in the border regions of Armenia. Originally sourced from factor.am.

title: It's unacceptable for us the more and more visible practice of the Russian peacekeeping troops to become a silent witness to the depopulation of Nagorno Karabakh - Pashinyan
content: Pashinyan stated that Armenia is a firm supporter of the presence of Russian peacekeeping troops in Nagorno-Karabakh, but it is unacceptable for them to become a silent witness to the depopulation of the region. He emphasized that the closure of the Lachin Corridor is a gross violation of point 6 of the tripartite statement of November 9, 2020.

title: Referring to the Almaty declaration does not mean that there will be no opportunity to refer to the status of Artsakh - Aghajanyan
content: Eduard Aghajanyan, Chairman of the Standing Committee on Foreign Relations, addressed Lavrov's statement regarding the Almaty Declaration and the question of the status of Nagorno-Karabakh. He stated that by referring to the Almaty declaration, no opportunity was lost, and it does not mean that there will not be an opportunity to address the status in the future. Originally sourced from armenpress.am.

title: Daily News Digest [29.10.2021]
content: PACE co-rapporteurs planned a fact-finding visit to Armenia in November 2021, with discussions focusing on electoral reform, judicial independence, the media situation, and the Nagorno-Karabakh conflict and its consequences on democratic institutions in Armenia.

title: Azerbaijan intends to close the Lachin Corridor - Alen Simonyan
content: Speaker Alen Simonyan stated that Armenia's task is to protect the population of Nagorno-Karabakh, noting that Azerbaijan had started announcing that the Nagorno-Karabakh problem no longer exists and demanded Russian peacekeepers stop using the name Nagorno-Karabakh and Armenian place names. He warned of Azerbaijan's intention to close the Lachin Corridor, calling it the only full-fledged road of humanitarian significance for the livelihood of the Armenians of Nagorno-Karabakh.

title: The talks about the Union state of Armenia with Russia and Belarus are premature - Narek Sukiasyan
content: Discussion of how Armenian political elites have framed the Armenia-Russia alliance. A sharp decline in Russian soft power polling in Armenia was attributed to the change in security environment and the defeat in the Second Karabakh War. Both Armenia and Azerbaijan viewed Moscow as the most important mediator or decision-maker in the Karabakh conflict.

title: Armenian universities remain under political control - Infocom investigation
content: Despite promises to eliminate political influence from universities, Armenia's government has maintained significant control over the country's higher education institutions. The investigation found that 8 out of 12 chairs of state university boards of trustees in Yerevan are political appointees, with three being ruling party members. This contrasts with PM Pashinyan's 2018 pledge that no party member should have the right to be a university rector and that universities should be 100% self-governing. 47% of the PM's appointees have political exposure, while an additional 18.3% of ministerial appointees are either state officials or connected to the ruling party.

title: They said and they did: Suspicious transactions and unknown origin of funds of pre-election donations of Civil Contract Party
content: Infocom.am published an investigative article alleging that funds of unknown origin were transferred through Armeconombank to support the election campaign of the Civil Contract Party. Armeconombank responded that the merely conditional data provided in the said publication are highly insufficient for initiating such an investigation.

title: Four Years Since the Second Nagorno-Karabakh War: A Data-Driven Reflection on Escalations Between Armenia and Azerbaijan
content: A data-driven analysis noting that the region continues to witness significant escalations, and the confrontation unfolds not only on the ground but also in the information space. The analysis revealed that Azerbaijan significantly outpaces Armenia in terms of information delivery, especially during pivotal events, with most peaks in news coverage dominated by Azerbaijan.

title: Fact-checking: Vigen Sargsyan and his wife really bought a private house in Belmont
content: Fact-checking article by Anna Sahakyan of infocom.am verifying that Vigen Sargsyan and his wife purchased a private house in Belmont. This article won 2nd place at Hetq's fact-checking journalism award ceremony in Armenia.

title: Legislative preparation, violation of the law, formal de-partisanship
content: Fact-checking article by Milena Khachikyan of infocom.am examining legislative preparation issues, legal violations, and formal de-partisanship processes. This article won 3rd place at Hetq's fact-checking journalism award ceremony in Armenia.

title: "Armenia" (Hayastan) Alliance coverage
content: Coverage of the Armenia (Hayastan) alliance founded in 2021 by Robert Kocharyan, the Armenian Revolutionary Federation, and the Reviving Armenia parties to participate in the snap parliamentary elections. Includes opposition movement activities and debates around Armenia-Hungary diplomatic relations.

title: Daily News Summary - Putin-Pashinyan telephone conversation on Nagorno-Karabakh
content: Coverage of a Putin-Pashinyan telephone conversation discussing the humanitarian crisis in Nagorno-Karabakh resulting from Azerbaijan's illegal blockade of the Lachin Corridor. Also covers the EU-Armenia Parliamentary Partnership Committee statement noting satisfaction with EU-Armenia relations.

title: Ministry of High-Tech Industry Archives
content: Archive page covering Armenia's Ministry of High-Tech Industry, which develops and implements government policy in the fields of communication, informatization, information technologies, information security, postal services, licensing, and the defense industry.

title: UITE (Union of Advanced Technology Enterprises) Archives
content: Archive covering the Union of Advanced Technology Enterprises (UITE), a business association uniting IT and high-tech companies, international corporations, organizations, and institutions operating in Armenia. Strategic directions include education, workforce development, Armenian IT brand creation, and defense industry.

title: EEU (Eurasian Economic Union) Archives
content: Archive page covering the Eurasian Economic Union, an international integration economic union founded on May 29, 2014, and in force since January 1, 2015. Articles dated from January 24, 2025 back through September-October 2024.

title: World Bank Archives
content: Archive page covering World Bank-related news in Armenia. Articles from March 2024 through February 13, 2025.

title: Donald Trump Archives
content: Archive page covering news related to Donald Trump, the 45th President of the United States, and relevant connections to Armenian affairs.

title: Parliamentary Committee on Science, Education, Culture, Diaspora, Youth and Sports Archives
content: Archive page covering the Standing Committee on Science, Education, Culture, Diaspora, Youth and Sports of the Armenian Parliament. Articles from January 10, 2025 and December 31, 2024.

title: Urban Development Committee Archives
content: Archive page covering the Urban Development Committee of Armenia with extensive coverage throughout 2024, from March through January 13, 2025, with numerous articles on construction and urban planning topics.

title: Standing Committee on Defense and Security Archives
content: Archive page covering the Standing Committee on Defense and Security of the Armenian Parliament. Articles spanning from May 2024 through January 10, 2025, covering defense, national security, police, and emergency matters.

title: State Supervisory Service Archives
content: Archive page covering the State Supervisory Service of Armenia. Articles from January 2024 through February 3, 2025, regarding state oversight activities.

title: October 27 Archives
content: Archive page related to the October 27, 1999 Armenian parliament shooting. Articles from 2024 and into May 2025 covering anniversary events and related discussions.

title: Informed Citizens United Archives
content: Archive page covering the Informed Citizens United organization, a fact-checking and civic organization. Articles from February 2024 through March 24, 2025.

title: Tigran Abrahamyan Archives
content: Archive page covering Tigran Abrahamyan, the I Have Honor faction MP. Articles from May 2024 through February 13, 2025.

title: Women's Support Center Archives
content: Archive page covering the Women's Support Center, a professional team of 15 people working to prevent and respond to domestic violence in Armenia.

title: Television and Radio Commission Archives
content: Archive page covering the Television and Radio Commission of Armenia, established based on a law adopted on October 9, 2000, which regulates broadcasting in Armenia.

title: Anna Grigoryan Archives
content: Archive page covering articles by or about Anna Grigoryan on infocom.am.

title: USA Archives
content: Archive page covering news about the United States. Describes the US as a federal constitutional republic in North America composed of 50 states with membership in international organizations including the UN Security Council, NATO, WTO, and OECD.

title: Republican Party of Armenia on ICC membership
content: The Republican Party claimed it is political short-sightedness to think that joining the International Criminal Court can protect Armenia's interests, calling it extreme political blindness fraught with unpredictable consequences.

title: Pinned Articles
content: Curated collection of pinned/featured articles on infocom.am covering key topics in Armenian news.

title: Fact-checking section
content: Infocom.am's dedicated fact-checking section covering verification of claims made in Armenian public discourse.
